name: thalamus
description: Thalamus — relay and gating between cortical columns. Every cortical L5→L4 connection passes through a thalamic relay. GPi controls relay state (inhibited by default, disinhibited by Go pathway). Reticular nucleus creates competition between relays.

nodes.description: Thalamic relay for PFC stripe 0. Passes signals from operation columns to PFC WM0 when disinhibited by GPi.
Relay for addition column output. Routes add results to PFC.
Thalamic reticular nucleus for stripe 0. Inhibitory — creates competition between relays. When relay_0 is active, TRN_0 inhibits neighboring relays.

edges.description: Active relay excites its reticular neuron.
Reticular inhibition: active relay suppresses neighbors.

properties.default_state: Relays are inhibited by GPi (via connections specified in config.json). When Go pathway disinhibits a relay, signals pass through to PFC.
properties.reticular_function: The TRN creates winner-take-all competition. When one relay opens, it suppresses the others. This focuses attention — only the most relevant input reaches PFC at any given moment.
